articles.headline: Government Unveils 'Digital India 2.0' with Focus on AI Integration and Rural Digital Literacy
articles.explainer:
• Prime Minister launched the second phase of the Digital India programme, aiming to bridge the urban-rural digital divide.
• Key pillars include AI for public services, enhanced cybersecurity, and a nationwide digital skilling initiative.
• Focus on leveraging Artificial Intelligence to improve governance, healthcare, and agriculture.
• Introduction of 'Gramin Digital Shiksha Abhiyan' to empower rural communities with essential digital skills.
• Emphasizes public-private partnerships for infrastructure development and innovation.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Information and Communication Technology (ICT)
E-governance
prelimsPoints: Digital India 2.0 launched on July 3, 2025.
Focus areas: AI integration, cybersecurity, rural digital literacy.
'Gramin Digital Shiksha Abhiyan' is a new component.
Aims to achieve 100% digital literacy in rural areas by 2030.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of Digital India and its impact on governance and service delivery.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities presented by integrating AI into public services in India.
Examine the role of digital literacy in achieving inclusive growth and bridging the urban-rural divide.
Evaluate the potential for public-private partnerships in scaling digital infrastructure and services.
articles.imageDescription: Prime Minister addressing the nation during the launch of Digital India 2.0, with digital graphics in the background.
articles.trivia: The original Digital India programme was launched on July 1, 2015, making this launch a decade after its inception.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% Amidst Inflation Concerns, Focus on Liquidity Management
articles.explainer:
• Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive time.
• Decision driven by persistent concerns over elevated food inflation and global economic uncertainties.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth.
• Governor highlighted the importance of dynamic liquidity management to ensure financial stability.
• Forecasts for GDP growth and inflation for FY26 were also updated, showing cautious optimism.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning
mobilization of resources
growth
development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: Repo rate maintained at 6.5% by RBI MPC.
This is the sixth consecutive time the rate has been unchanged.
Primary reason: elevated food inflation and global economic uncertainties.
RBI's mandate includes maintaining price stability while keeping growth in mind.
MPC consists of six members (3 RBI, 3 GOI nominees).
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth in the current global scenario.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools, particularly the repo rate, in managing inflation in India.
Examine the impact of persistent food inflation on household budgets and overall economic stability.
Evaluate the role of liquidity management by the RBI in ensuring financial sector stability.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building in Mumbai with a graph showing stable interest rates.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee was constituted in 2016 and is entrusted with the task of fixing the benchmark policy interest rate (repo rate).

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Key Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark judgment, largely upheld the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The ruling dismissed several petitions challenging aspects of the Act related to state access to data and broad exemptions.
• Court emphasized the need for a robust data protection framework in the digital age, balancing individual privacy with national security.
• Directed the government to formulate clearer guidelines for 'legitimate uses' of data by state agencies.
• This judgment provides legal certainty for businesses and individuals regarding data privacy in India.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Information and Communication Technology (ICT)
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court upheld major provisions of DPDP Act, 2023.
Act aims to regulate the processing of digital personal data.
Right to Privacy is a fundamental right under Article 21 (Puttaswamy judgment).
The ruling provides clarity on data protection laws in India.
Challenges were primarily regarding state access and exemptions.
mainsPoints: Analyze the balance struck by the DPDP Act, 2023, between individual privacy and legitimate state interests, in light of the Supreme Court's judgment.
Discuss the implications of a robust data protection framework on India's digital economy and international data flows.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing data protection laws effectively in a diverse and digitally evolving nation like India.
Examine the concept of 'Right to be Forgotten' and other key rights granted to data principals under the Act.
articles.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building facade, symbolizing justice and legal pronouncements.
articles.trivia: India's data protection journey gained significant momentum after the S. Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared privacy a fundamental right.

articles.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Emphasizes Indo-Pacific Security and Economic Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, reaffirming commitment to a free and open Indo-Pacific.
• Discussions focused on enhancing defense cooperation, maritime security, and supply chain resilience.
• Both nations pledged to deepen collaboration in emerging technologies, clean energy, and critical minerals.
• Joint statement highlighted concerns over regional stability and underscored the importance of multilateralism.
• India's 'Act East' policy and Japan's 'Free and Open Indo-Pacific' vision were central to the discussions.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue held in New Delhi.
Key focus: Indo-Pacific security, defense, economic cooperation.
Both countries share 'Free and Open Indo-Pacific' vision.
Cooperation areas include clean energy, critical minerals, emerging technologies.
Strategic partnership is crucial for regional stability.
mainsPoints: Analyze the growing strategic convergence between India and Japan in the context of Indo-Pacific security dynamics.
Discuss the significance of the India-Japan partnership for regional stability and economic resilience.
Examine how India's 'Act East' policy aligns with Japan's foreign policy objectives in Southeast Asia.
Evaluate the potential for enhanced cooperation in critical and emerging technologies between the two nations.
articles.imageDescription: Foreign Ministers of India and Japan shaking hands during the strategic dialogue.
articles.trivia: India and Japan are also members of the Quadrilateral Security Dialogue (Quad), along with the USA and Australia.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Cryogenic Engine for Next-Gen Launch Vehicle (NGLV)
articles.explainer:
• Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful long-duration hot test of a new high-thrust cryogenic engine.
• This engine is critical for the development of India's Next-Generation Launch Vehicle (NGLV), designed for heavier payloads and future space missions.
• NGLV aims to replace the PSLV and GSLV series, offering cost-effective and reusable launch capabilities.
• The test marks a significant milestone in India's quest for advanced space launch technology and self-reliance.
• Future applications include human spaceflight, deep space missions, and satellite constellations.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space technology
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tested a cryogenic engine for NGLV.
NGLV is designed for heavier payloads and future missions.
Aims to replace PSLV and GSLV with reusable capabilities.
Cryogenic engines use liquid hydrogen and liquid oxygen as propellants.
This is a key step towards India's advanced space capabilities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of developing the Next-Generation Launch Vehicle (NGLV) for India's future space ambitions.
Analyze the technological challenges and strategic advantages of cryogenic propulsion in space launch systems.
Examine how NGLV can enhance India's capabilities in human spaceflight, deep space missions, and commercial launches.
Evaluate the role of indigenous space technology in achieving self-reliance and global leadership in space exploration.
articles.imageDescription: An illustration of ISRO's Next-Generation Launch Vehicle (NGLV) during launch.
articles.trivia: India is one of only a handful of countries to have mastered cryogenic engine technology, a complex and challenging area of rocket science.

articles.headline: Union Health Ministry Drafts New National Health Policy 2025, Focus on Preventative Care
articles.explainer:
• The Union Health Ministry released the draft National Health Policy 2025 for public consultation, aiming for universal health coverage.
• The policy emphasizes a paradigm shift from curative to preventative and promotive healthcare.
• Proposes significant increase in public health expenditure, targeting 3% of GDP by 2030.
• Focuses on strengthening primary healthcare, digital health infrastructure, and addressing mental health challenges.
• Aims to integrate traditional Indian medicine systems (AYUSH) into mainstream healthcare delivery.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health
Education
Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: Draft National Health Policy 2025 released for public consultation.
Key focus: preventative and promotive healthcare.
Target public health expenditure: 3% of GDP by 2030.
Aims to strengthen primary healthcare and digital health.
Integration of AYUSH systems is a major objective.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the proposed National Health Policy 2025, highlighting its strengths and potential challenges in implementation.
Discuss the importance of shifting focus from curative to preventative healthcare in achieving universal health coverage in India.
Examine the role of increased public health expenditure and digital health infrastructure in improving health outcomes.
Evaluate the potential of integrating AYUSH systems into mainstream healthcare delivery for holistic well-being.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting various aspects of healthcare, including doctors, patients, and digital health symbols.
articles.trivia: India's previous National Health Policy was formulated in 2017, which aimed to achieve the highest possible level of health and well-being for all through a comprehensive healthcare system.

articles.headline: Southwest Monsoon Progresses Steadily; Boosts Kharif Sowing Prospects
articles.explainer:
• The India Meteorological Department (IMD) reported a steady and widespread advance of the Southwest Monsoon across key agricultural regions.
• Timely and adequate rainfall has significantly boosted prospects for Kharif crop sowing, especially for rice, pulses, and oilseeds.
• Reservoir levels are showing improvement, which is crucial for irrigation and power generation.
• IMD predicts normal to above-normal rainfall for most parts of the country in July.
• Government agencies are monitoring sowing activities and ensuring availability of fertilizers and seeds.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of world’s physical geography
Distribution of key natural resources across the world
Factors responsible for the location of primary, secondary, and tertiary sector industries in various parts of the world
prelimsPoints: Southwest Monsoon progressing steadily across India.
Favorable for Kharif crop sowing (rice, pulses, oilseeds).
IMD predicts normal to above-normal rainfall for July.
Improved reservoir levels are beneficial for irrigation.
Kharif crops are sown at the beginning of the monsoon season.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical role of the Southwest Monsoon in India's agricultural economy and food security.
Analyze the impact of monsoon variability on crop production and farmer livelihoods.
Examine the government's strategies to mitigate risks associated with monsoon fluctuations and ensure agricultural resilience.
Evaluate the importance of accurate meteorological forecasts for agricultural planning and disaster management.
articles.imageDescription: Farmers working in a green paddy field under monsoon clouds.
articles.trivia: The Southwest Monsoon accounts for nearly 75% of India's annual rainfall and is crucial for the country's agriculture-dependent economy.

articles.headline: Government Revises FDI Policy to Attract Investment in Green Hydrogen Sector
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry announced revisions to the Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) policy, specifically targeting the green hydrogen sector.
• The changes aim to streamline approvals and offer incentives for foreign companies investing in green hydrogen production and related infrastructure.
• This move is part of India's larger National Green Hydrogen Mission to become a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
• Expected to boost domestic manufacturing, create jobs, and accelerate India's transition to a clean energy economy.
• The policy revision signals India's commitment to achieving its climate change goals and energy independence.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Investment models
Infrastructure: Energy
Environmental pollution and degradation
prelimsPoints: FDI policy revised for green hydrogen sector.
Aims to attract foreign investment in green hydrogen production.
Part of National Green Hydrogen Mission.
India targets to become a global hub for green hydrogen.
Green hydrogen is produced using renewable energy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate change commitments.
Analyze how revisions in FDI policy can catalyze investment and technological advancements in the green hydrogen sector.
Examine the economic and environmental benefits of becoming a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
Identify the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and infrastructure in India.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized image of a green hydrogen production plant with wind turbines in the background.
articles.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, generating no greenhouse gas emissions in the process.

articles.headline: New Tiger Census Report Shows Continued Growth in India's Big Cat Population
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change released the latest All India Tiger Estimation (Tiger Census) report.
• The report indicates a healthy and sustained growth in India's wild tiger population, reinforcing conservation efforts.
• Advanced methodologies, including camera trapping and DNA analysis, were used for the estimation.
• Highlights successful implementation of Project Tiger and the need for continuous habitat protection.
• Emphasizes challenges like human-wildlife conflict and poaching, requiring integrated conservation strategies.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: Latest All India Tiger Estimation report released.
Shows continued growth in India's tiger population.
Advanced methods like camera trapping and DNA analysis used.
Project Tiger launched in 1973 for tiger conservation.
India is home to over 70% of the world's wild tiger population.
mainsPoints: Discuss the success story of Project Tiger in India and the factors contributing to the growth in tiger population.
Analyze the challenges that persist in tiger conservation, including human-wildlife conflict and habitat fragmentation.
Examine the importance of tiger conservation for maintaining ecological balance and biodiversity.
Evaluate the role of community participation and technological advancements in effective wildlife management.
articles.imageDescription: A majestic Bengal tiger captured by a camera trap in a dense forest.
articles.trivia: India achieved the St. Petersburg Declaration goal of doubling its tiger population by 2022, four years ahead of schedule.

articles.headline: Government Proposes New National Cybersecurity Framework to Combat Evolving Threats
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released a draft National Cybersecurity Framework for public feedback.
• Aims to strengthen India's digital infrastructure against sophisticated cyberattacks and data breaches.
• Proposes mandatory reporting of cyber incidents, enhanced collaboration between public and private sectors, and critical infrastructure protection.
• Focuses on skill development in cybersecurity and fostering indigenous solutions.
• The framework seeks to align with global best practices while addressing India-specific challenges.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Organized crime and terrorism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
Cyber security
prelimsPoints: Draft National Cybersecurity Framework released by MeitY.
Aims to protect digital infrastructure from cyber threats.
Proposes mandatory incident reporting and public-private collaboration.
Focus on skill development in cybersecurity.
CERT-In is India's national nodal agency for responding to cyber incidents.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing threats to India's cybersecurity and the need for a comprehensive national framework.
Analyze the key provisions of the proposed National Cybersecurity Framework and their potential impact on digital security.
Examine the challenges in implementing a robust cybersecurity framework, including skill gaps and cross-border cooperation.
Evaluate the importance of public-private partnerships and international collaboration in combating cybercrime and enhancing cyber resilience.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized image representing cybersecurity with network lines and lock symbols.
articles.trivia: India's Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) functions as the national agency to perform various functions in the area of cyber security.

articles.headline: India and Brazil Discuss Preferential Trade Agreement Expansion
articles.explainer:
• India and Brazil held high-level discussions to explore the expansion of their existing Preferential Trade Agreement (PTA).
• The aim is to diversify trade baskets and reduce tariff barriers on a wider range of goods.
• Focus areas include agricultural products, pharmaceuticals, automotive components, and digital services.
• Both nations are keen to strengthen South-South cooperation and leverage their roles in BRICS and IBSA.
• An expanded PTA is expected to significantly boost bilateral trade and investment flows.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India and Brazil discussing expansion of PTA.
Aims to diversify trade and reduce tariffs.
Focus on agriculture, pharma, auto components, digital services.
Both are members of BRICS and IBSA.
PTA allows preferential access to certain products.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential benefits of expanding the Preferential Trade Agreement between India and Brazil for both economies.
Discuss the role of South-South cooperation, particularly through forums like BRICS and IBSA, in shaping global economic dynamics.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in diversifying India's trade basket with Latin American countries.
Evaluate the strategic importance of Brazil as a partner for India in terms of food security and resource availability.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Brazil intertwined, symbolizing bilateral cooperation.
articles.trivia: India and Mercosur (a South American trade bloc including Brazil) have a Preferential Trade Agreement in place since 2009.

articles.headline: Ministry of Tourism Launches 'Virasat Darshan' Initiative for Experiential Heritage Tourism
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism launched 'Virasat Darshan', a new initiative to promote experiential heritage tourism across India.
• Focuses on developing circuits connecting lesser-known historical sites with cultural experiences, local cuisine, and artisan crafts.
• Aims to boost local economies, generate employment, and create awareness about India's rich cultural heritage.
• Utilizes digital platforms for immersive virtual tours and multilingual guides.
• Part of the 'Dekho Apna Desh' campaign, encouraging domestic tourism and sustainable practices.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Salient features of Indian Society
prelimsPoints: 'Virasat Darshan' initiative launched by Ministry of Tourism.
Aims to promote experiential heritage tourism.
Connects lesser-known historical sites and cultural experiences.
Part of 'Dekho Apna Desh' campaign.
Focuses on local economy and cultural preservation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of heritage tourism in fostering cultural preservation, economic development, and national integration in India.
Analyze how initiatives like 'Virasat Darshan' can contribute to sustainable tourism practices and empower local communities.
Examine the role of digital technology in enhancing the reach and appeal of heritage tourism in India.
Evaluate the challenges in managing and promoting lesser-known heritage sites while ensuring their conservation.
articles.imageDescription: Tourists exploring an ancient temple complex, reflecting India's rich heritage.
articles.trivia: India is home to 42 UNESCO World Heritage Sites (as of 2023), reflecting its diverse natural and cultural heritage.

articles.headline: PM e-Bus Seva Scheme to Expand to 200 More Cities, Promoting Green Mobility
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the expansion of the 'PM-eBus Seva' scheme to cover an additional 200 cities across India.
• Aims to deploy 10,000 new electric buses, further strengthening public transport and reducing carbon emissions.
• The scheme operates on a public-private partnership (PPP) model, with central government providing financial assistance.
• Focuses on cities lacking organized bus services and promoting charging infrastructure.
• Part of India's commitment to sustainable urban mobility and achieving Net Zero targets.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Environmental pollution and degradation
prelimsPoints: PM-eBus Seva scheme expanded to 200 more cities.
Aims to deploy 10,000 new electric buses.
Operates on a Public-Private Partnership (PPP) model.
Promotes green mobility and reduces carbon emissions.
Focuses on cities needing better public transport.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the PM-eBus Seva scheme in promoting sustainable urban mobility and reducing air pollution in Indian cities.
Analyze the effectiveness of the Public-Private Partnership (PPP) model in scaling up electric vehicle adoption in public transport.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in developing robust charging infrastructure for electric buses across diverse urban landscapes.
Evaluate India's progress and commitments towards achieving Net Zero targets through initiatives like green public transport.
articles.imageDescription: A fleet of modern electric buses parked at a depot in an urban setting.
articles.trivia: India aims to achieve 30% electric vehicle penetration in private cars, 70% in commercial vehicles, and 80% in two and three-wheelers by 2030.

articles.headline: India Plans Fourth Research Station in Antarctica to Enhance Polar Studies
articles.explainer:
• India announced plans to establish its fourth permanent research station in Antarctica, reinforcing its commitment to polar science.
• The new station aims to facilitate advanced research in climate change, glaciology, oceanography, and atmospheric sciences.
• Will expand India's logistical and scientific capabilities in the Antarctic region, complementing existing stations (Maitri, Bharati).
• Emphasizes international collaboration under the Antarctic Treaty System for peaceful scientific exploration.
• This move underscores India's growing role in global scientific endeavors and environmental monitoring.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Environment and Ecology
keyTerms: National Centre for Polar and Ocean Research (NCPOR)
prelimsPoints: India plans its fourth research station in Antarctica.
Current operational stations: Maitri and Bharati.
Dakshin Gangotri was India's first station.
New station for climate change, glaciology, oceanography research.
Antarctic Treaty System governs activities in Antarctica.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic and scientific importance of India's polar research program in Antarctica.
Analyze how India's presence in Antarctica contributes to global understanding of climate change and environmental processes.
Examine the challenges involved in establishing and maintaining research stations in extreme polar environments.
Evaluate the principles and significance of the Antarctic Treaty System in promoting peaceful scientific cooperation.
articles.imageDescription: An aerial view of an Indian research station nestled amidst snow-covered Antarctic landscape.
articles.trivia: India became a signatory to the Antarctic Treaty in 1983 and holds a consultative status.

articles.headline: NCERT Finalizes New National Curriculum Framework for School Education
articles.explainer:
• The National Council of Educational Research and Training (NCERT) announced the finalization of the new National Curriculum Framework (NCF) for school education.
• The NCF, aligned with the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, aims for holistic development, critical thinking, and multidisciplinary learning.
• Introduces new pedagogical approaches, assessment reforms, and emphasis on foundational literacy and numeracy.
• Focuses on reducing curriculum load, promoting experiential learning, and integrating vocational education from early stages.
• Implementation of the new textbooks based on this framework is expected from the upcoming academic session.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Education
Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: NCERT finalized new National Curriculum Framework (NCF).
Aligned with National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
Aims for holistic development, critical thinking.
Focus on reducing curriculum load, experiential learning.
New textbooks based on NCF expected soon.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key reforms introduced by the new National Curriculum Framework (NCF) and its potential impact on school education in India.
Analyze how the NCF aligns with the vision of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 to transform the Indian education system.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in implementing new pedagogical approaches and assessment reforms across diverse educational settings.
Evaluate the importance of foundational literacy, numeracy, and vocational integration in creating a future-ready workforce.
articles.imageDescription: Students engaged in a collaborative learning activity in a modern classroom setting.
articles.trivia: The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 is the third such policy in India's history, after those in 1968 and 1986.

articles.headline: G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors Meet to Discuss Global Economic Stability
articles.explainer:
• Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors from G20 nations convened to address pressing global economic challenges.
• Discussions centered on inflation management, debt sustainability for developing nations, and strengthening global financial architecture.
• India reiterated its call for reformed multilateral development banks and inclusive global governance.
• Focus also on climate finance, digital public infrastructure, and cross-border payments.
• The meeting laid groundwork for upcoming G20 Leaders' Summit, emphasizing coordinated policy responses.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors meeting.
Discussed global economic stability, inflation, debt.
India advocated for reformed multilateral development banks.
Other topics: climate finance, digital public infrastructure.
G20 represents 85% of global GDP.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role of the G20 in addressing contemporary global economic challenges such as inflation and debt sustainability.
Discuss India's position and contributions to G20 discussions, particularly regarding reforms of multilateral development banks.
Examine the importance of international cooperation in managing global economic shocks and fostering sustainable growth.
Evaluate the significance of climate finance and digital public infrastructure as key agenda items in global economic forums.
articles.imageDescription: Delegates from various G20 nations seated around a conference table during a meeting.
articles.trivia: The G20 was formed in 1999 in response to the Asian financial crisis as a forum for finance ministers and central bank governors.

articles.headline: Scientists Discover New Plant and Amphibian Species in Western Ghats
articles.explainer:
• Researchers from the Zoological Survey of India (ZSI) and Botanical Survey of India (BSI) announced the discovery of several new species.
• The discoveries include a new species of frog and a rare flowering plant, found in the biodiversity hotspot of the Western Ghats.
• Highlights the immense, unexplored biodiversity of the Western Ghats and the urgent need for its conservation.
• Genetic analysis confirmed the distinctiveness of these species, adding to India's rich natural heritage.
• The findings underscore the impact of climate change and habitat loss on sensitive ecosystems.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: New species of frog and flowering plant discovered in Western Ghats.
Discoveries by ZSI and BSI.
Western Ghats is a recognized biodiversity hotspot.
Highlights rich, unexplored biodiversity.
Species are endemic to the region.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the threats it faces.
Analyze the importance of discovering new species for scientific research and conservation efforts.
Examine the role of organizations like ZSI and BSI in documenting India's biodiversity.
Evaluate the impact of climate change and human activities on the fragile ecosystems of the Western Ghats.
articles.imageDescription: A close-up photograph of a newly discovered vibrant green frog on a leaf in the Western Ghats.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and are recognized as one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.

articles.headline: Debate Intensifies Over Delays in Judicial Appointments to High Courts
articles.explainer:
• The ongoing debate regarding delays in judicial appointments to various High Courts has intensified.
• Concerns raised by legal experts and the Supreme Court itself about the impact on justice delivery and case backlogs.
• Highlights the friction between the Collegium system for judicial appointments and the government's role.
• Advocates for a more transparent and time-bound mechanism for filling judicial vacancies.
• The issue underscores the need for reforms to ensure efficiency and independence of the judiciary.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Appointment to various Constitutional Posts, Powers, Functions and Responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
prelimsPoints: Debate over delays in High Court judicial appointments.
Concerns about justice delivery and case backlogs.
Collegium system recommends appointments.
Government's role in the appointment process.
High Courts have jurisdiction over states/UTs.
mainsPoints: Critically examine the issues surrounding delays in judicial appointments to High Courts and their implications for the justice delivery system in India.
Discuss the functioning of the Collegium system for judicial appointments and the arguments for and against its efficacy.
Analyze the challenges arising from the interplay between the judiciary and the executive in the appointment process.
Propose reforms to ensure transparency, accountability, and timely filling of judicial vacancies.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel and law books on a wooden desk, symbolizing the judicial system.
articles.trivia: The concept of the Collegium system evolved through a series of Supreme Court judgments, not through an Act of Parliament or a Constitutional amendment.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Launches Pilot Project on AI-Powered Smart Farming in Drought-Prone Regions
articles.explainer:
• NITI Aayog, in collaboration with agricultural universities and tech firms, launched a pilot project on AI-powered smart farming.
• The project aims to enhance agricultural productivity and resilience in drought-prone regions using artificial intelligence.
• Involves precision agriculture techniques, real-time soil and weather monitoring, and AI-driven crop advisories.
• Focuses on optimizing water usage, predicting pest outbreaks, and improving yield forecasting for small and marginal farmers.
• Expected to serve as a model for scaling up technology adoption in Indian agriculture.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country
e-technology in the aid of farmers
Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices
Technology missions
Animal-rearing economics
prelimsPoints: NITI Aayog launched AI-powered smart farming pilot project.
Focus on drought-prone regions.
Uses AI for precision agriculture, real-time monitoring.
Aims to optimize water use, predict pests, improve yields.
Collaborates with agricultural universities and tech firms.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and smart farming techniques in transforming Indian agriculture, particularly in drought-prone areas.
Analyze the benefits and challenges of adopting precision agriculture for small and marginal farmers in India.
Examine the role of government bodies like NITI Aayog in fostering technological innovation in the agricultural sector.
Evaluate how AI-driven solutions can contribute to food security and climate resilience in agriculture.
articles.imageDescription: A drone flying over a field, symbolizing modern smart farming techniques.
articles.trivia: NITI Aayog replaced the Planning Commission in 2015, serving as a policy 'think tank' of the Government of India.

articles.headline: ASI Undertakes Massive Restoration Project for Ancient Temples in Southern India
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) initiated a comprehensive restoration project for several ancient temple complexes in Southern India.
• The multi-year project focuses on structural conservation, intricate sculpture restoration, and scientific preservation techniques.
• Aims to protect and promote India's rich architectural heritage, particularly those impacted by weather and time.
• Involves collaboration with local communities and heritage experts to ensure authentic restoration.
• The initiative underscores the government's commitment to safeguarding cultural treasures for future generations.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Salient features of Indian Society
prelimsPoints: ASI launched restoration project for ancient temples in Southern India.
Focus on structural conservation and sculpture restoration.
Aims to protect architectural heritage.
Involves collaboration with local communities.
ASI is under the Ministry of Culture.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of cultural heritage preservation for national identity and tourism in India.
Analyze the challenges faced by the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in conserving ancient monuments across the country.
Examine the scientific techniques and community involvement crucial for effective restoration projects.
Evaluate the economic and social benefits of investing in the preservation of architectural heritage.
articles.imageDescription: Artisans meticulously restoring intricate carvings on an ancient South Indian temple wall.
articles.trivia: The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) was founded in 1861 by Alexander Cunningham, who also became its first Director-General.

highlights: Government launches 'Digital India 2.0' focusing on AI integration and rural digital literacy.
RBI maintains repo rate at 6.5% for sixth consecutive time amidst inflation concerns.
Supreme Court upholds key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023.
India-Japan Strategic Dialogue emphasizes Indo-Pacific security and economic cooperation.
New Tiger Census Report shows continued growth in India's big cat population, reinforcing conservation efforts.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the recently launched 'Digital India 2.0' initiative:
1. It primarily focuses on urban digital infrastructure development.
2. 'Gramin Digital Shiksha Abhiyan' is a key component aimed at rural digital literacy.
3. It emphasizes leveraging Artificial Intelligence for public services.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: Digital India 2.0 aims to bridge the urban-rural digital divide, with a significant focus on rural areas. Statement 2 is correct: 'Gramin Digital Shiksha Abhiyan' is introduced for rural digital literacy. Statement 3 is correct: A key pillar of the initiative is AI integration for public services.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy, consider the following statements:
1. The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has kept the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive time.
2. The decision was primarily driven by concerns over global economic uncertainties and elevated food inflation.
3. The MPC consists of five members, with three nominated by the Government of India and two from the RBI.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The RBI MPC maintained the repo rate at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive time. Statement 2 is correct: Elevated food inflation and global economic uncertainties were key reasons. Statement 3 is incorrect: The MPC consists of six members – three from the RBI and three nominated by the Government of India.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act, 2023, and the recent Supreme Court judgment is/are correct?
1. The Supreme Court largely upheld the constitutional validity of the DPDP Act, 2023.
2. The Act aims to regulate the processing of digital personal data in India.
3. The Right to Privacy was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court in the S. Puttaswamy judgment in 2017.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The Supreme Court upheld the DPDP Act, 2023, which regulates digital personal data. The Right to Privacy was indeed declared a fundamental right in the Puttaswamy judgment.

prelimsMocks.question:
Regarding the latest All India Tiger Estimation (Tiger Census) report, consider the following:
1. India is home to over 70% of the world's wild tiger population.
2. The report indicated a decrease in the tiger population due to habitat fragmentation.
3. Project Tiger was launched in 1973 for tiger conservation.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: India hosts over 70% of the world's wild tiger population. Statement 2 is incorrect: The report showed a healthy and sustained growth in the tiger population. Statement 3 is correct: Project Tiger was launched in 1973.

prelimsMocks.question: The draft National Health Policy 2025, recently released by the Union Health Ministry, proposes a significant increase in public health expenditure to what percentage of GDP by 2030?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The draft National Health Policy 2025 proposes to increase public health expenditure to 3% of GDP by 2030, aiming for universal health coverage and a shift towards preventative care.

mainsMocks.question: The launch of 'Digital India 2.0' signifies India's commitment to leveraging technology for inclusive growth. Critically analyze its potential to bridge the digital divide and transform public service delivery, considering both opportunities and challenges.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors; E-governance
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce Digital India 2.0 and its objectives. Discuss opportunities like improved access to services, transparency, economic empowerment, and AI integration. Address challenges such as infrastructure gaps, digital literacy, data privacy, and cybersecurity. Conclude with recommendations for effective implementation and inclusive outcomes.

mainsMocks.question: The proposed National Health Policy 2025 emphasizes a shift towards preventative and promotive healthcare while targeting increased public health expenditure. Discuss the implications of this policy shift for India's healthcare system and the potential role of integrating AYUSH systems in achieving universal health coverage.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health; Government policies and interventions
mainsMocks.approach: Explain the current state of India's healthcare. Detail the policy's focus on preventative care and increased expenditure, outlining benefits like reduced disease burden and improved public health. Analyze the challenges of implementation (funding, infrastructure, human resources). Discuss how AYUSH integration can offer cost-effective, holistic care and expand reach, while also considering integration challenges.

mainsMocks.question: India's consistent growth in tiger population, as highlighted by the latest census report, is a testament to successful conservation efforts. However, significant challenges persist. Discuss the factors contributing to this success and critically examine the remaining threats to tiger conservation in India.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Biodiversity
mainsMocks.approach: Begin with the success story of Project Tiger and the latest census findings. Identify success factors: protected areas, anti-poaching measures, community involvement, scientific monitoring. Then, delve into persistent challenges: human-wildlife conflict, habitat fragmentation, climate change impacts, illegal wildlife trade, and resource constraints. Conclude with a forward-looking statement on integrated conservation strategies.

quickBytes.text: Digital India 2.0 aims for 100% rural digital literacy by 2030 through 'Gramin Digital Shiksha Abhiyan'.
RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) comprises 6 members (3 from RBI, 3 GOI nominees).
The Right to Privacy was declared a fundamental right under Article 21 by the Supreme Court in 2017.
India and Japan are key partners in the Quadrilateral Security Dialogue (Quad) along with USA and Australia.
Cryogenic engines use liquid hydrogen and liquid oxygen as propellants, critical for NGLV.
The Southwest Monsoon accounts for nearly 75% of India's annual rainfall.
Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water using renewable electricity, with zero emissions.
India achieved the St. Petersburg Declaration goal of doubling tiger population by 2022, 4 years early.
India's Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) is the national nodal agency for cyber incidents.
NITI Aayog, a policy 'think tank', replaced the Planning Commission in 2015.

GS1.headline: Southwest Monsoon Progresses Steadily; Boosts Kharif Sowing Prospects
GS1.explainer:
• The India Meteorological Department (IMD) reported a steady and widespread advance of the Southwest Monsoon across key agricultural regions.
• Timely and adequate rainfall has significantly boosted prospects for Kharif crop sowing, especially for rice, pulses, and oilseeds.
• Reservoir levels are showing improvement, which is crucial for irrigation and power generation.
• IMD predicts normal to above-normal rainfall for most parts of the country in July.
• Government agencies are monitoring sowing activities and ensuring availability of fertilizers and seeds.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of world’s physical geography
Distribution of key natural resources across the world
Factors responsible for the location of primary, secondary, and tertiary sector industries in various parts of the world
prelimsPoints: Southwest Monsoon progressing steadily across India.
Favorable for Kharif crop sowing (rice, pulses, oilseeds).
IMD predicts normal to above-normal rainfall for July.
Improved reservoir levels are beneficial for irrigation.
Kharif crops are sown at the beginning of the monsoon season.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical role of the Southwest Monsoon in India's agricultural economy and food security.
Analyze the impact of monsoon variability on crop production and farmer livelihoods.
Examine the government's strategies to mitigate risks associated with monsoon fluctuations and ensure agricultural resilience.
Evaluate the importance of accurate meteorological forecasts for agricultural planning and disaster management.
GS1.imageDescription: Farmers working in a green paddy field under monsoon clouds.
GS1.trivia: The Southwest Monsoon accounts for nearly 75% of India's annual rainfall and is crucial for the country's agriculture-dependent economy.

GS1.headline: Ministry of Tourism Launches 'Virasat Darshan' Initiative for Experiential Heritage Tourism
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism launched 'Virasat Darshan', a new initiative to promote experiential heritage tourism across India.
• Focuses on developing circuits connecting lesser-known historical sites with cultural experiences, local cuisine, and artisan crafts.
• Aims to boost local economies, generate employment, and create awareness about India's rich cultural heritage.
• Utilizes digital platforms for immersive virtual tours and multilingual guides.
• Part of the 'Dekho Apna Desh' campaign, encouraging domestic tourism and sustainable practices.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Salient features of Indian Society
prelimsPoints: 'Virasat Darshan' initiative launched by Ministry of Tourism.
Aims to promote experiential heritage tourism.
Connects lesser-known historical sites and cultural experiences.
Part of 'Dekho Apna Desh' campaign.
Focuses on local economy and cultural preservation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of heritage tourism in fostering cultural preservation, economic development, and national integration in India.
Analyze how initiatives like 'Virasat Darshan' can contribute to sustainable tourism practices and empower local communities.
Examine the role of digital technology in enhancing the reach and appeal of heritage tourism in India.
Evaluate the challenges in managing and promoting lesser-known heritage sites while ensuring their conservation.
GS1.imageDescription: Tourists exploring an ancient temple complex, reflecting India's rich heritage.
GS1.trivia: India is home to 42 UNESCO World Heritage Sites (as of 2023), reflecting its diverse natural and cultural heritage.

GS1.headline: ASI Undertakes Massive Restoration Project for Ancient Temples in Southern India
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) initiated a comprehensive restoration project for several ancient temple complexes in Southern India.
• The multi-year project focuses on structural conservation, intricate sculpture restoration, and scientific preservation techniques.
• Aims to protect and promote India's rich architectural heritage, particularly those impacted by weather and time.
• Involves collaboration with local communities and heritage experts to ensure authentic restoration.
• The initiative underscores the government's commitment to safeguarding cultural treasures for future generations.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Salient features of Indian Society
prelimsPoints: ASI launched restoration project for ancient temples in Southern India.
Focus on structural conservation and sculpture restoration.
Aims to protect architectural heritage.
Involves collaboration with local communities.
ASI is under the Ministry of Culture.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of cultural heritage preservation for national identity and tourism in India.
Analyze the challenges faced by the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in conserving ancient monuments across the country.
Examine the scientific techniques and community involvement crucial for effective restoration projects.
Evaluate the economic and social benefits of investing in the preservation of architectural heritage.
GS1.imageDescription: Artisans meticulously restoring intricate carvings on an ancient South Indian temple wall.
GS1.trivia: The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) was founded in 1861 by Alexander Cunningham, who also became its first Director-General.

GS2.headline: Government Unveils 'Digital India 2.0' with Focus on AI Integration and Rural Digital Literacy
GS2.explainer:
• Prime Minister launched the second phase of the Digital India programme, aiming to bridge the urban-rural digital divide.
• Key pillars include AI for public services, enhanced cybersecurity, and a nationwide digital skilling initiative.
• Focus on leveraging Artificial Intelligence to improve governance, healthcare, and agriculture.
• Introduction of 'Gramin Digital Shiksha Abhiyan' to empower rural communities with essential digital skills.
• Emphasizes public-private partnerships for infrastructure development and innovation.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Information and Communication Technology (ICT)
E-governance
prelimsPoints: Digital India 2.0 launched on July 3, 2025.
Focus areas: AI integration, cybersecurity, rural digital literacy.
'Gramin Digital Shiksha Abhiyan' is a new component.
Aims to achieve 100% digital literacy in rural areas by 2030.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of Digital India and its impact on governance and service delivery.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities presented by integrating AI into public services in India.
Examine the role of digital literacy in achieving inclusive growth and bridging the urban-rural divide.
Evaluate the potential for public-private partnerships in scaling digital infrastructure and services.
GS2.imageDescription: Prime Minister addressing the nation during the launch of Digital India 2.0, with digital graphics in the background.
GS2.trivia: The original Digital India programme was launched on July 1, 2015, making this launch a decade after its inception.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Key Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark judgment, largely upheld the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The ruling dismissed several petitions challenging aspects of the Act related to state access to data and broad exemptions.
• Court emphasized the need for a robust data protection framework in the digital age, balancing individual privacy with national security.
• Directed the government to formulate clearer guidelines for 'legitimate uses' of data by state agencies.
• This judgment provides legal certainty for businesses and individuals regarding data privacy in India.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Information and Communication Technology (ICT)
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court upheld major provisions of DPDP Act, 2023.
Act aims to regulate the processing of digital personal data.
Right to Privacy is a fundamental right under Article 21 (Puttaswamy judgment).
The ruling provides clarity on data protection laws in India.
Challenges were primarily regarding state access and exemptions.
mainsPoints: Analyze the balance struck by the DPDP Act, 2023, between individual privacy and legitimate state interests, in light of the Supreme Court's judgment.
Discuss the implications of a robust data protection framework on India's digital economy and international data flows.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing data protection laws effectively in a diverse and digitally evolving nation like India.
Examine the concept of 'Right to be Forgotten' and other key rights granted to data principals under the Act.
GS2.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building facade, symbolizing justice and legal pronouncements.
GS2.trivia: India's data protection journey gained significant momentum after the S. Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared privacy a fundamental right.

GS2.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Emphasizes Indo-Pacific Security and Economic Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, reaffirming commitment to a free and open Indo-Pacific.
• Discussions focused on enhancing defense cooperation, maritime security, and supply chain resilience.
• Both nations pledged to deepen collaboration in emerging technologies, clean energy, and critical minerals.
• Joint statement highlighted concerns over regional stability and underscored the importance of multilateralism.
• India's 'Act East' policy and Japan's 'Free and Open Indo-Pacific' vision were central to the discussions.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue held in New Delhi.
Key focus: Indo-Pacific security, defense, economic cooperation.
Both countries share 'Free and Open Indo-Pacific' vision.
Cooperation areas include clean energy, critical minerals, emerging technologies.
Strategic partnership is crucial for regional stability.
mainsPoints: Analyze the growing strategic convergence between India and Japan in the context of Indo-Pacific security dynamics.
Discuss the significance of the India-Japan partnership for regional stability and economic resilience.
Examine how India's 'Act East' policy aligns with Japan's foreign policy objectives in Southeast Asia.
Evaluate the potential for enhanced cooperation in critical and emerging technologies between the two nations.
GS2.imageDescription: Foreign Ministers of India and Japan shaking hands during the strategic dialogue.
GS2.trivia: India and Japan are also members of the Quadrilateral Security Dialogue (Quad), along with the USA and Australia.

GS2.headline: Union Health Ministry Drafts New National Health Policy 2025, Focus on Preventative Care
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Health Ministry released the draft National Health Policy 2025 for public consultation, aiming for universal health coverage.
• The policy emphasizes a paradigm shift from curative to preventative and promotive healthcare.
• Proposes significant increase in public health expenditure, targeting 3% of GDP by 2030.
• Focuses on strengthening primary healthcare, digital health infrastructure, and addressing mental health challenges.
• Aims to integrate traditional Indian medicine systems (AYUSH) into mainstream healthcare delivery.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health
Education
Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: Draft National Health Policy 2025 released for public consultation.
Key focus: preventative and promotive healthcare.
Target public health expenditure: 3% of GDP by 2030.
Aims to strengthen primary healthcare and digital health.
Integration of AYUSH systems is a major objective.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the proposed National Health Policy 2025, highlighting its strengths and potential challenges in implementation.
Discuss the importance of shifting focus from curative to preventative healthcare in achieving universal health coverage in India.
Examine the role of increased public health expenditure and digital health infrastructure in improving health outcomes.
Evaluate the potential of integrating AYUSH systems into mainstream healthcare delivery for holistic well-being.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic depicting various aspects of healthcare, including doctors, patients, and digital health symbols.
GS2.trivia: India's previous National Health Policy was formulated in 2017, which aimed to achieve the highest possible level of health and well-being for all through a comprehensive healthcare system.

GS2.headline: India and Brazil Discuss Preferential Trade Agreement Expansion
GS2.explainer:
• India and Brazil held high-level discussions to explore the expansion of their existing Preferential Trade Agreement (PTA).
• The aim is to diversify trade baskets and reduce tariff barriers on a wider range of goods.
• Focus areas include agricultural products, pharmaceuticals, automotive components, and digital services.
• Both nations are keen to strengthen South-South cooperation and leverage their roles in BRICS and IBSA.
• An expanded PTA is expected to significantly boost bilateral trade and investment flows.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India and Brazil discussing expansion of PTA.
Aims to diversify trade and reduce tariffs.
Focus on agriculture, pharma, auto components, digital services.
Both are members of BRICS and IBSA.
PTA allows preferential access to certain products.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential benefits of expanding the Preferential Trade Agreement between India and Brazil for both economies.
Discuss the role of South-South cooperation, particularly through forums like BRICS and IBSA, in shaping global economic dynamics.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in diversifying India's trade basket with Latin American countries.
Evaluate the strategic importance of Brazil as a partner for India in terms of food security and resource availability.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Brazil intertwined, symbolizing bilateral cooperation.
GS2.trivia: India and Mercosur (a South American trade bloc including Brazil) have a Preferential Trade Agreement in place since 2009.

GS2.headline: PM e-Bus Seva Scheme to Expand to 200 More Cities, Promoting Green Mobility
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the expansion of the 'PM-eBus Seva' scheme to cover an additional 200 cities across India.
• Aims to deploy 10,000 new electric buses, further strengthening public transport and reducing carbon emissions.
• The scheme operates on a public-private partnership (PPP) model, with central government providing financial assistance.
• Focuses on cities lacking organized bus services and promoting charging infrastructure.
• Part of India's commitment to sustainable urban mobility and achieving Net Zero targets.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Environmental pollution and degradation
prelimsPoints: PM-eBus Seva scheme expanded to 200 more cities.
Aims to deploy 10,000 new electric buses.
Operates on a Public-Private Partnership (PPP) model.
Promotes green mobility and reduces carbon emissions.
Focuses on cities needing better public transport.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the PM-eBus Seva scheme in promoting sustainable urban mobility and reducing air pollution in Indian cities.
Analyze the effectiveness of the Public-Private Partnership (PPP) model in scaling up electric vehicle adoption in public transport.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in developing robust charging infrastructure for electric buses across diverse urban landscapes.
Evaluate India's progress and commitments towards achieving Net Zero targets through initiatives like green public transport.
GS2.imageDescription: A fleet of modern electric buses parked at a depot in an urban setting.
GS2.trivia: India aims to achieve 30% electric vehicle penetration in private cars, 70% in commercial vehicles, and 80% in two and three-wheelers by 2030.

GS2.headline: NCERT Finalizes New National Curriculum Framework for School Education
GS2.explainer:
• The National Council of Educational Research and Training (NCERT) announced the finalization of the new National Curriculum Framework (NCF) for school education.
• The NCF, aligned with the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, aims for holistic development, critical thinking, and multidisciplinary learning.
• Introduces new pedagogical approaches, assessment reforms, and emphasis on foundational literacy and numeracy.
• Focuses on reducing curriculum load, promoting experiential learning, and integrating vocational education from early stages.
• Implementation of the new textbooks based on this framework is expected from the upcoming academic session.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Education
Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: NCERT finalized new National Curriculum Framework (NCF).
Aligned with National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
Aims for holistic development, critical thinking.
Focus on reducing curriculum load, experiential learning.
New textbooks based on NCF expected soon.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key reforms introduced by the new National Curriculum Framework (NCF) and its potential impact on school education in India.
Analyze how the NCF aligns with the vision of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 to transform the Indian education system.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in implementing new pedagogical approaches and assessment reforms across diverse educational settings.
Evaluate the importance of foundational literacy, numeracy, and vocational integration in creating a future-ready workforce.
GS2.imageDescription: Students engaged in a collaborative learning activity in a modern classroom setting.
GS2.trivia: The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 is the third such policy in India's history, after those in 1968 and 1986.

GS2.headline: G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors Meet to Discuss Global Economic Stability
GS2.explainer:
• Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors from G20 nations convened to address pressing global economic challenges.
• Discussions centered on inflation management, debt sustainability for developing nations, and strengthening global financial architecture.
• India reiterated its call for reformed multilateral development banks and inclusive global governance.
• Focus also on climate finance, digital public infrastructure, and cross-border payments.
• The meeting laid groundwork for upcoming G20 Leaders' Summit, emphasizing coordinated policy responses.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors meeting.
Discussed global economic stability, inflation, debt.
India advocated for reformed multilateral development banks.
Other topics: climate finance, digital public infrastructure.
G20 represents 85% of global GDP.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role of the G20 in addressing contemporary global economic challenges such as inflation and debt sustainability.
Discuss India's position and contributions to G20 discussions, particularly regarding reforms of multilateral development banks.
Examine the importance of international cooperation in managing global economic shocks and fostering sustainable growth.
Evaluate the significance of climate finance and digital public infrastructure as key agenda items in global economic forums.
GS2.imageDescription: Delegates from various G20 nations seated around a conference table during a meeting.
GS2.trivia: The G20 was formed in 1999 in response to the Asian financial crisis as a forum for finance ministers and central bank governors.

GS2.headline: Debate Intensifies Over Delays in Judicial Appointments to High Courts
GS2.explainer:
• The ongoing debate regarding delays in judicial appointments to various High Courts has intensified.
• Concerns raised by legal experts and the Supreme Court itself about the impact on justice delivery and case backlogs.
• Highlights the friction between the Collegium system for judicial appointments and the government's role.
• Advocates for a more transparent and time-bound mechanism for filling judicial vacancies.
• The issue underscores the need for reforms to ensure efficiency and independence of the judiciary.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Appointment to various Constitutional Posts, Powers, Functions and Responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
prelimsPoints: Debate over delays in High Court judicial appointments.
Concerns about justice delivery and case backlogs.
Collegium system recommends appointments.
Government's role in the appointment process.
High Courts have jurisdiction over states/UTs.
mainsPoints: Critically examine the issues surrounding delays in judicial appointments to High Courts and their implications for the justice delivery system in India.
Discuss the functioning of the Collegium system for judicial appointments and the arguments for and against its efficacy.
Analyze the challenges arising from the interplay between the judiciary and the executive in the appointment process.
Propose reforms to ensure transparency, accountability, and timely filling of judicial vacancies.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel and law books on a wooden desk, symbolizing the judicial system.
GS2.trivia: The concept of the Collegium system evolved through a series of Supreme Court judgments, not through an Act of Parliament or a Constitutional amendment.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% Amidst Inflation Concerns, Focus on Liquidity Management
GS3.explainer:
• Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive time.
• Decision driven by persistent concerns over elevated food inflation and global economic uncertainties.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth.
• Governor highlighted the importance of dynamic liquidity management to ensure financial stability.
• Forecasts for GDP growth and inflation for FY26 were also updated, showing cautious optimism.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning
mobilization of resources
growth
development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: Repo rate maintained at 6.5% by RBI MPC.
This is the sixth consecutive time the rate has been unchanged.
Primary reason: elevated food inflation and global economic uncertainties.
RBI's mandate includes maintaining price stability while keeping growth in mind.
MPC consists of six members (3 RBI, 3 GOI nominees).
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth in the current global scenario.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools, particularly the repo rate, in managing inflation in India.
Examine the impact of persistent food inflation on household budgets and overall economic stability.
Evaluate the role of liquidity management by the RBI in ensuring financial sector stability.
GS3.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building in Mumbai with a graph showing stable interest rates.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee was constituted in 2016 and is entrusted with the task of fixing the benchmark policy interest rate (repo rate).

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Cryogenic Engine for Next-Gen Launch Vehicle (NGLV)
GS3.explainer:
• Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful long-duration hot test of a new high-thrust cryogenic engine.
• This engine is critical for the development of India's Next-Generation Launch Vehicle (NGLV), designed for heavier payloads and future space missions.
• NGLV aims to replace the PSLV and GSLV series, offering cost-effective and reusable launch capabilities.
• The test marks a significant milestone in India's quest for advanced space launch technology and self-reliance.
• Future applications include human spaceflight, deep space missions, and satellite constellations.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space technology
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tested a cryogenic engine for NGLV.
NGLV is designed for heavier payloads and future missions.
Aims to replace PSLV and GSLV with reusable capabilities.
Cryogenic engines use liquid hydrogen and liquid oxygen as propellants.
This is a key step towards India's advanced space capabilities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of developing the Next-Generation Launch Vehicle (NGLV) for India's future space ambitions.
Analyze the technological challenges and strategic advantages of cryogenic propulsion in space launch systems.
Examine how NGLV can enhance India's capabilities in human spaceflight, deep space missions, and commercial launches.
Evaluate the role of indigenous space technology in achieving self-reliance and global leadership in space exploration.
GS3.imageDescription: An illustration of ISRO's Next-Generation Launch Vehicle (NGLV) during launch.
GS3.trivia: India is one of only a handful of countries to have mastered cryogenic engine technology, a complex and challenging area of rocket science.

GS3.headline: Government Revises FDI Policy to Attract Investment in Green Hydrogen Sector
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry announced revisions to the Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) policy, specifically targeting the green hydrogen sector.
• The changes aim to streamline approvals and offer incentives for foreign companies investing in green hydrogen production and related infrastructure.
• This move is part of India's larger National Green Hydrogen Mission to become a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
• Expected to boost domestic manufacturing, create jobs, and accelerate India's transition to a clean energy economy.
• The policy revision signals India's commitment to achieving its climate change goals and energy independence.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Investment models
Infrastructure: Energy
Environmental pollution and degradation
prelimsPoints: FDI policy revised for green hydrogen sector.
Aims to attract foreign investment in green hydrogen production.
Part of National Green Hydrogen Mission.
India targets to become a global hub for green hydrogen.
Green hydrogen is produced using renewable energy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate change commitments.
Analyze how revisions in FDI policy can catalyze investment and technological advancements in the green hydrogen sector.
Examine the economic and environmental benefits of becoming a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
Identify the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and infrastructure in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized image of a green hydrogen production plant with wind turbines in the background.
GS3.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, generating no greenhouse gas emissions in the process.

GS3.headline: New Tiger Census Report Shows Continued Growth in India's Big Cat Population
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change released the latest All India Tiger Estimation (Tiger Census) report.
• The report indicates a healthy and sustained growth in India's wild tiger population, reinforcing conservation efforts.
• Advanced methodologies, including camera trapping and DNA analysis, were used for the estimation.
• Highlights successful implementation of Project Tiger and the need for continuous habitat protection.
• Emphasizes challenges like human-wildlife conflict and poaching, requiring integrated conservation strategies.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: Latest All India Tiger Estimation report released.
Shows continued growth in India's tiger population.
Advanced methods like camera trapping and DNA analysis used.
Project Tiger launched in 1973 for tiger conservation.
India is home to over 70% of the world's wild tiger population.
mainsPoints: Discuss the success story of Project Tiger in India and the factors contributing to the growth in tiger population.
Analyze the challenges that persist in tiger conservation, including human-wildlife conflict and habitat fragmentation.
Examine the importance of tiger conservation for maintaining ecological balance and biodiversity.
Evaluate the role of community participation and technological advancements in effective wildlife management.
GS3.imageDescription: A majestic Bengal tiger captured by a camera trap in a dense forest.
GS3.trivia: India achieved the St. Petersburg Declaration goal of doubling its tiger population by 2022, four years ahead of schedule.

GS3.headline: Government Proposes New National Cybersecurity Framework to Combat Evolving Threats
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released a draft National Cybersecurity Framework for public feedback.
• Aims to strengthen India's digital infrastructure against sophisticated cyberattacks and data breaches.
• Proposes mandatory reporting of cyber incidents, enhanced collaboration between public and private sectors, and critical infrastructure protection.
• Focuses on skill development in cybersecurity and fostering indigenous solutions.
• The framework seeks to align with global best practices while addressing India-specific challenges.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Organized crime and terrorism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
Cyber security
prelimsPoints: Draft National Cybersecurity Framework released by MeitY.
Aims to protect digital infrastructure from cyber threats.
Proposes mandatory incident reporting and public-private collaboration.
Focus on skill development in cybersecurity.
CERT-In is India's national nodal agency for responding to cyber incidents.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing threats to India's cybersecurity and the need for a comprehensive national framework.
Analyze the key provisions of the proposed National Cybersecurity Framework and their potential impact on digital security.
Examine the challenges in implementing a robust cybersecurity framework, including skill gaps and cross-border cooperation.
Evaluate the importance of public-private partnerships and international collaboration in combating cybercrime and enhancing cyber resilience.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized image representing cybersecurity with network lines and lock symbols.
GS3.trivia: India's Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) functions as the national agency to perform various functions in the area of cyber security.

GS3.headline: India Plans Fourth Research Station in Antarctica to Enhance Polar Studies
GS3.explainer:
• India announced plans to establish its fourth permanent research station in Antarctica, reinforcing its commitment to polar science.
• The new station aims to facilitate advanced research in climate change, glaciology, oceanography, and atmospheric sciences.
• Will expand India's logistical and scientific capabilities in the Antarctic region, complementing existing stations (Maitri, Bharati).
• Emphasizes international collaboration under the Antarctic Treaty System for peaceful scientific exploration.
• This move underscores India's growing role in global scientific endeavors and environmental monitoring.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Environment and Ecology
keyTerms: National Centre for Polar and Ocean Research (NCPOR)
prelimsPoints: India plans its fourth research station in Antarctica.
Current operational stations: Maitri and Bharati.
Dakshin Gangotri was India's first station.
New station for climate change, glaciology, oceanography research.
Antarctic Treaty System governs activities in Antarctica.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic and scientific importance of India's polar research program in Antarctica.
Analyze how India's presence in Antarctica contributes to global understanding of climate change and environmental processes.
Examine the challenges involved in establishing and maintaining research stations in extreme polar environments.
Evaluate the principles and significance of the Antarctic Treaty System in promoting peaceful scientific cooperation.
GS3.imageDescription: An aerial view of an Indian research station nestled amidst snow-covered Antarctic landscape.
GS3.trivia: India became a signatory to the Antarctic Treaty in 1983 and holds a consultative status.

GS3.headline: Scientists Discover New Plant and Amphibian Species in Western Ghats
GS3.explainer:
• Researchers from the Zoological Survey of India (ZSI) and Botanical Survey of India (BSI) announced the discovery of several new species.
• The discoveries include a new species of frog and a rare flowering plant, found in the biodiversity hotspot of the Western Ghats.
• Highlights the immense, unexplored biodiversity of the Western Ghats and the urgent need for its conservation.
• Genetic analysis confirmed the distinctiveness of these species, adding to India's rich natural heritage.
• The findings underscore the impact of climate change and habitat loss on sensitive ecosystems.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: New species of frog and flowering plant discovered in Western Ghats.
Discoveries by ZSI and BSI.
Western Ghats is a recognized biodiversity hotspot.
Highlights rich, unexplored biodiversity.
Species are endemic to the region.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the threats it faces.
Analyze the importance of discovering new species for scientific research and conservation efforts.
Examine the role of organizations like ZSI and BSI in documenting India's biodiversity.
Evaluate the impact of climate change and human activities on the fragile ecosystems of the Western Ghats.
GS3.imageDescription: A close-up photograph of a newly discovered vibrant green frog on a leaf in the Western Ghats.
GS3.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and are recognized as one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.

GS3.headline: NITI Aayog Launches Pilot Project on AI-Powered Smart Farming in Drought-Prone Regions
GS3.explainer:
• NITI Aayog, in collaboration with agricultural universities and tech firms, launched a pilot project on AI-powered smart farming.
• The project aims to enhance agricultural productivity and resilience in drought-prone regions using artificial intelligence.
• Involves precision agriculture techniques, real-time soil and weather monitoring, and AI-driven crop advisories.
• Focuses on optimizing water usage, predicting pest outbreaks, and improving yield forecasting for small and marginal farmers.
• Expected to serve as a model for scaling up technology adoption in Indian agriculture.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country
e-technology in the aid of farmers
Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices
Technology missions
Animal-rearing economics
prelimsPoints: NITI Aayog launched AI-powered smart farming pilot project.
Focus on drought-prone regions.
Uses AI for precision agriculture, real-time monitoring.
Aims to optimize water use, predict pests, improve yields.
Collaborates with agricultural universities and tech firms.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and smart farming techniques in transforming Indian agriculture, particularly in drought-prone areas.
Analyze the benefits and challenges of adopting precision agriculture for small and marginal farmers in India.
Examine the role of government bodies like NITI Aayog in fostering technological innovation in the agricultural sector.
Evaluate how AI-driven solutions can contribute to food security and climate resilience in agriculture.
GS3.imageDescription: A drone flying over a field, symbolizing modern smart farming techniques.
GS3.trivia: NITI Aayog replaced the Planning Commission in 2015, serving as a policy 'think tank' of the Government of India.
